name: zscaler_private_access
explanation: Zscaler Private Access is a vendor SaaS security tile. The Datadog integration is logs-only: the spec defines a single `logs` block expecting the user to declare a TCP listener (`type: tcp`, `port: <PORT>`) for the Zscaler appliance to forward syslog to. There is no Agent metric check (`datadog_checks/zscaler_private_access/` has no `.py` check module — only `__about__.py`, `__init__.py`, and `data/conf.yaml.example`). The Agent does not initiate any connection toward Zscaler; it passively receives forwarded events on a user-chosen port that must also be configured upstream in Zscaler. There is no probeable endpoint and no shared secret/credential that would even fit the `credentials-required` shape — the missing input is a logging pipeline both sides must be wired up for.
notes: Logs-only vendor SaaS integration. Same shape as wazuh and zeek; pattern N variant for vendor security tiles.
